calibrated_on: Bonn segments, 4097 samples (S2 confirmatory: combined AR-null FPR 0.020)

epoch_length_specificity_guard.rationale: SampEn lower-tail finite-N anti-conservative bias is length-dependent; the alpha/2 correction was calibrated for ~4097-sample segments and is NOT predeclared valid below that length.

evidence.note: AR-null (linear) epochs SURVIVED ~37.5% at p<=0.025 on 501-sample BNCI epochs -> specificity broken; far above the 0.05 gate. Raw probe, not the verdict, but consistent with finite-N theory.

why_not_run: Running the full 9-subject confirmatory would produce a foreknown specificity failure; calling it NOT_PASSED would misattribute a method-transfer limitation to the dataset. Re-tuning the finite-N rule to pass on short epochs after seeing the probe is FORBIDDEN (post-hoc tuning).
why_not_passed: No BNCI claim; instrument not validated for 501-sample epochs.
next_method: Pre-register an epoch-length-calibrated finite-N rule (e.g. length-scaled threshold or FDR-on-AR-null specificity), validate it on a held-out short-epoch null BEFORE any BNCI confirmatory.
